metadata.name: greeting-responder
metadata.description: Respond to user greetings with a friendly, contextual message. Use when the user says hello, hi, hey, good morning, or similar greetings.

execution_flow.trigger: User sends a greeting like hello, hi, hey, or good morning

steps.action: Detect the greeting type (formal vs casual)
Match the tone in the response
Ask what the user needs help with

outputs: A friendly greeting response matching the user's tone

file_analysis.purpose: Main skill definition with greeting response instructions

mermaid_sequence:
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant C as Claude
    U->>C: Greeting message
    C->>C: Detect greeting type
    C->>C: Match tone
    C->>U: Friendly response + offer help
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart TD
    A[User sends greeting] --> B{Formal or casual?}
    B -->|Formal| C[Respond formally]
    B -->|Casual| D[Respond casually]
    C --> E[Ask how to help]
    D --> E
summary: A simple greeting skill that detects the tone of user greetings and responds in kind. It matches formal or casual language and follows up by asking how it can assist the user.
deep_dive:
## Overview

The greeting-responder skill is a minimal, single-file skill with no external dependencies or tool usage.

## Execution Flow

When triggered by a user greeting, the skill follows a three-step process:
1. Detect whether the greeting is formal or casual
2. Match the detected tone in the response
3. Ask the user what they need help with

There is no error handling, script execution, or external interaction — this is purely a conversational skill.
